file: 2018 HS ICDC Tests and Keys (all clusters).pdf
title: ICDC 2018 (Marketing)

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of how laws enact social change:

options.A: You cannot use illegal substances without facing serious consequences.
options.B: If you invent something, you can keep others from stealing your idea by filing a patent.
options.C: If someone vandalizes a park, s/he will be punished with fines or jail time.
options.D: Strict laws fell upon the tobacco industry when people became angry about its harmful effects.

questions.explanation: Strict laws fell upon the tobacco industry when people became angry about its harmful effects. Laws are vehicles for enacting social change and are often passed in response to shifts in public opinion as well as social movements. When people became angry about cigarettes and their harmful health effects, the government responded to this shift in public opinion by passing laws that limit tobacco companies in various way. Patents, punishments for vandalizing, and serious consequences for using illegal substances are not examples of how laws can enact social change. Regulations)
sources: SOURCE: LAP-BL-163—Laying Down the Law (Complying With the Spirit and Intent of Laws and

questions.question: One of the primary purposes of customs regulations is to

options.A: standardize a country's higher education system.
options.B: prevent illegal materials from exiting or entering a country.
options.C: allow terrorists to access a country's top-secret information.
options.D: make it more difficult for illegal immigration to occur.

questions.explanation: Prevent illegal materials from exiting or entering a country. One of the primary purposes of customs regulations is to protect a country's borders from illegal materials such as illicit drugs, weapons, or other dangerous items. Customs regulations are not put in place to standardize a country's higher education system or to make illegal immigration more difficult. Customs regulations are implemented to protect a country from terrorists, not to allow them to access sensitive information. https://www.cbp.gov/about
sources: SOURCE: U.S. Customs and Border Protection. (2017). About CBP. Retrieved August 10, 2017, from

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of good organizational design within a company:
questions.explanation: Improved communication. Organizational design helps communication to flow smoothly within a business because employees know exactly whom they should talk to when they have questions or problems. Good organizational design also creates clear lines of authority and increases innovation and productivity.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-EC-103—Designed to Work (Organizational Design of Businesses)

questions.question: One common barrier to the use of technology in channel management is

options.A: the possibility for increased productivity and efficiency.
options.B: companies' refusal to implement new tools.
options.C: the overwhelming number of technology options.
options.D: employees' inability to master new technology.

questions.explanation: The overwhelming number of technology options. Using technology is a great way to improve overall channel management success. However, the sheer number of options for channel management technologies can be overwhelming for many companies. This is a common barrier to implementing channel management technology. Companies generally don't refuse to implement new tools because most companies understand the value of new technology. The possibility for increased productivity and efficiency is a benefit of using channel management technology, not a barrier to it. Employees are generally able and willing to learn to master new technologies. technology landscape. Retrieved September 25, 2017, from http://info.zs.com/techbytes/seven-steps-to-navigate-the-channel-management-technology- landscape
sources: SOURCE: DeSarbo, J. (2016, December 14). Seven steps to navigate the channel management

questions.question: The producer of HandiFresh home cleaning supplies recently informed QuickMart discount stores that they must sell the entire catalog of HandiFresh products in their stores or HandiFresh won't allow QuickMart to sell any HandiFresh products at all. This is an example of
questions.explanation: Full-line forcing. Full-line forcing is an illegal business practice in which a producer or supplier demands that a seller carries the full range of the producer's products. By demanding that QuickMart must sell the entire catalog of HandiFresh products, HandiFresh engaged in full-line forcing. A tying agreement is an agreement in which a producer conditions the sale of one product on the buyer's agreement to purchase a separate product. Exclusive dealing is when a distributor agrees to sell products from a single manufacturer. There is no such thing as product coercion in channel management. http://www.mbaskool.com/business-concepts/marketing-and-strategy-terms/11180-full-line- forcing.html 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 14
sources: SOURCE: MBASkool.com. (2011-2017). Full line forcing. Retrieved September 22, 2017, from

questions.question: FreshFast Grocery Store charges food manufacturers an annual fee for the ability to sell their products at FreshFast stores. In other words, FreshFast requires the food manufacturers to pay a
questions.explanation: Slotting allowance. A slotting allowance, or a slotting fee, is a sum paid by a manufacturer to a retail store for making room for its products on the store shelves. Many individuals consider charging a slotting allowance unethical, although it is usually legally permissible. This arrangement is not referred to as a product placement fee, a distribution charge, or a supermarket fee. Retrieved October 3, 2017, from https://www.repsly.com/blog/consumer-goods/slotting-fee- negotiation-strategy
sources: SOURCE: Vessella, V. (2017, April 25). Slotting fees: 3 ways to get your product on the shelf for less.

questions.question: Main Street Grocery and Food Emporium are located approximately 10 miles away from each other. Each grocery chain sells Nature Oasis bread in its stores. Food Emporium, however, recently slashed prices on all of its inventory of Nature Oasis products in an effort to bring in more customers. The leaders of Main Street Grocery are angry because ever since Food Emporium cut prices on Nature Oasis, Main Street Grocery has experienced declining sales on bread products. This situation could be described as __________ conflict.
questions.explanation: Horizontal. Conflicts can ensue when members of a distribution channel disagree. When channel members at the same level within the channel of distribution, such as Main Street Grocery and Food Emporium, have a disagreement, horizontal conflict exists. Vertical conflicts involve a disagreement among channel members of different levels. Sideways conflict and backwards conflict are not terms used to describe channel conflicts. from http://smallbusiness.chron.com/horizontal-vertical-marketing-conflicts-65325.html
sources: SOURCE: Mack, S. (2017). Horizontal & vertical marketing conflicts. Retrieved September 22, 2017,

questions.question: People's Bank recently partnered with a local supermarket chain to offer a special rewards credit card for supermarket customers. Both the bank and the supermarket hope to gain more customers from the relationship. This is an example of

options.D: dual promotion. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 2

questions.explanation: Affinity marketing. Affinity marketing occurs when two companies link their brands for a mutually beneficial outcome. The relationship between People's Bank and the local supermarket chain is an example of an affinity partner relationship. Mutual advertising, company partnership, and dual promotion are not terms used to describe this type of relationship. http://www.marketing-schools.org/types-of-marketing/affinity-marketing.html
sources: SOURCE: Marketing-Schools.org. (2017). Affinity marketing. Retrieved September 22, 2017, from

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an internal distraction that could disrupt the listening process:
questions.explanation: A headache. An internal distraction is a concern or worry on the part of the listener that takes away from his/her ability to attend to the speaker's message, such as a headache, hunger or thirst, a personal problem, or poor health. A ringing cell phone, other people talking, or a television are all examples of environmental distractions, which are aspects of the setting that divert the listener's attention away from the speaker's message.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-CO-017—Listen Up! (Demonstrating Active Listening Skills)

questions.question: When should a visual aid be shown during a presentation?

options.A: At the end of the presentation to summarize
options.B: During the entire presentation to maintain interest
options.C: When the speaker shares relevant information
options.D: When the audience gets bored and restless

questions.explanation: When the speaker shares relevant information. Speakers should show visual aids to enhance their message when those charts, graphics, videos, etc. correspond with the specific information that they are presenting. It's not likely that an image will be relevant or appropriate for the entire presentation, and overuse may cause it to lose its impact. A visual aid might help engage a bored audience but would add little value if it isn't related to the material being shared. While visual aids may assist a speaker in summarizing his/her presentation, visual aids can also be helpful during the introduction or body of a presentation.
sources: SOURCE: LAP CO-025—Well Said! (Making Oral Presentations)

questions.question: Infographics, heat maps, and dynamic model outputs are all examples of __________ tools.
questions.explanation: Data visualization. Infographics, heat maps, and dynamic model outputs are all examples of tools that can be used to display data in an easily understandable way. They are not examples of personal health tools, accounting tools, or public relations tools. 2017, from http://www.creativebloq.com/design-tools/data-visualization-712402 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 15
sources: SOURCE: Suda, B. (2017, February 7). The 38 best tools for data visualization. Retrieved August 10,

questions.question: When posting on your company's social media page, it is important to

options.A: make aggressive attacks on your competitors' products.
options.B: say whatever is on your mind at the current moment.
options.C: avoid posting any content that is new or interesting.
options.D: ensure that your message aligns with company values.

questions.explanation: Ensure that your message aligns with company values. The messages that you post on your company's social media page should align with company values. If a message is posted that conflicts with organizational values, the company may face public backlash, legal action, or angry customers. People posting on company-run social media pages should not say whatever is on their mind or make aggressive attacks on competitors' products. Both actions would be inappropriate and potentially harmful to the company. It is important, however, to post content that is new and interesting on company-run social media pages. Retrieved August 10, 2017, from http://www.cleveland.com/business/index.ssf/2014/02/tips_on_avoiding_social_media_faux- pas_in_the_workplace.html
sources: SOURCE: Cho, J. (2014, February 9). Tips on avoiding social media faux-pas in the workplace.

questions.question: Which of the following is a reason to build rapport with customers:

options.D: To find out a customer's information

questions.explanation: To create positive connections. Rapport is a level of trust and understanding between a company and a customer. It is important to build rapport to create positive connections with customers so that they feel comfortable making a purchase. Companies do not build rapport with customers to take advantage of them, to make them feel intimidated, or to find out their personal information. Retrieved August 10, 2017, from http://www.inspirationalsolutions- nlp.co.uk/theimportanceofrapport.pdf
sources: SOURCE: Inspirational Solutions. (2017). What is rapport and why is it important to build rapport?

questions.question: Which of the following is a true statement regarding data integrity and security:

options.A: Data security programs are strictly monitored by the government.
options.B: Businesses are not allowed to sell customer information.
options.C: It can be helpful to hire a data security specialist.
options.D: All businesses have similar data security needs.

questions.explanation: It can be helpful to hire a data security specialist. It can be helpful for a business to hire a data security specialist to help it design and implement a data security plan. Businesses are allowed to sell customer information as long as they state this intention in their privacy policies. Data security programs are not monitored by the government. Data security needs vary from industry to industry.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-CR-017—Trust Is a Must (Ethics in Customer Relationship Management)

questions.question: Opening a restaurant in New York City is more expensive than in a small town in Upstate New York. This is a result of limited
questions.explanation: Natural resources. In a highly populated are like New York City, land is more limited than in a small town, so people are willing to pay more to use the land. In a small town, the land is less limited, so it is less expensive. Human resources, capital goods, and consumer goods are not necessarily more or less limited in either New York City or a small town.

questions.question: Benjamin's job involves monitoring the various retailers that sell his company's products. He analyzes each retailer's sales reports so that his company can see which stores are most successful. Benjamin's job falls under the category of
questions.explanation: Channel management. Channel management is a set of processes by which marketers ensure that products are distributed to customers efficiently and effectively. Part of channel management involves analyzing a company's channels of distributions, or the paths and routes that goods take from the producer to the customer. Ben's job, therefore, falls under the category of channel management—not managerial accounting, financial planning, or project management. Managerial accounting is the process of analyzing financial information for the pursuit of an organization's goals. Financial planning involves making a comprehensive evaluation of a client's financial state and then recommending appropriate actions. Project management involves planning and executing a project with the intent of achieving a specific goal or outcome. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 16

questions.question: To calculate net profit for a business, which of the following is subtracted from gross profit:

options.D: Advertising costs 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 3

questions.explanation: Operating expenses. To determine net profit, operating expenses are subtracted from gross profit. Cost of goods sold is subtracted from income to determine gross profit. Employee wages and advertising costs are both individual operating expenses, but subtracting them alone will not result in an accurate calculation of net profit.

questions.question: The federal government recently lowered taxes and interest rates, causing businesses and consumers to spend more money. Which phase of the business cycle is the economy most likely to be experiencing?
questions.explanation: Expansion. Governments often lower taxes and interest rates to encourage consumers and producers to spend and stimulate the economy, resulting in an economic expansion. The contraction and trough phases of the business cycle occur when economic activities decline, not when businesses and consumers spend more money. Investment, which is the use of money to generate a profit or gain, often increases during economic expansion, but it is not a phase of the business cycle.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-EC-009—Boom or Bust (Impact of Business Cycles)

questions.question: Your friend Meg recently ordered a gift for her mother from a specialty clothing company in Italy. Meg is participating in the business activity of
questions.explanation: Global trade. Global trade takes place when a product is produced in one country and consumed in another. Your friend Meg is participating in global trade because she purchased a gift for her mother that was made in Italy. Meg is not participating in the business activity of exporting because she is not producing and shipping goods to another country for profit. She is not participating in international affairs, since she is a regular citizen with no political office. She is not engaging in trade talks, which are economic summits used to improve trade relations among countries.

questions.question: Emotionally intelligent leaders display
questions.explanation: Confidence. Emotionally intelligent leaders display confidence when they know they are doing or saying the right thing. Emotional intelligence doesn't necessarily relate to technical expertise or authority. Emotionally intelligent leaders may or may not need to display hopefulness—it depends on the situation.

questions.question: Following the rules

options.A: is not necessary for responsible behavior.

questions.explanation: Helps society to run smoothly. Following the rules helps society to run smoothly and is an essential part of responsible behavior. Everyone should follow the rules. Following the rules doesn't necessarily ensure that you will succeed, but it is the responsible, right thing to do.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-EI-021—Make the Honor Role (Acting Responsibly)

questions.question: Brandon's best friend Trevor just won a scholarship competition that Brandon also competed for. Brandon should show empathy by

options.A: expressing that he is happy and excited for Trevor's success.
options.B: sharing his real feelings of disappointment with Trevor.
options.C: telling Trevor he knows how grueling the competition was.
options.D: inviting Trevor and his family to his big graduation party.

questions.explanation: Expressing that he is happy and excited for Trevor's success. Brandon can show empathy for Trevor by celebrating Trevor's success and by giving supportive feedback to him that shows that Brandon is happy for him. Sharing his own disappointment, talking about the competition, or inviting Trevor to his graduation party do not allow Brandon to demonstrate empathy for the excitement that Trevor is feeling
sources: SOURCE: LAP-EI-030—Have a Heart (Showing Empathy for Others)

questions.question: Senders can create barriers to effective communication when they
questions.explanation: Use poor grammar. Using poor grammar, spelling, or syntax is a barrier to effective communication because it can be distracting and can prevent the receiver from taking the sender seriously. Being friendly and polite is not a barrier to effective communication. Disagreements are not necessarily barriers to effective communication, as long as both the sender and receiver remain respectful and open during the communication. Failure to actively listen is a barrier to effective communication on the part of the receiver, not the sender. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 17
sources: SOURCE: LAP-EI-140—More Than Just Talk (Effective Communication)

questions.question: Roxy, who works in human resource management, needs to resolve a conflict between two coworkers. Which of the following is the correct sequence of steps for Roxy to take:

options.A: Discuss possible resolutions, confront the conflict, acknowledge the conflict, define the conflict, and define the resolution
options.B: Define the resolution, define the conflict, acknowledge the conflict, confront the conflict, and discuss possible resolutions
options.C: Acknowledge the conflict, define the conflict, confront the conflict, discuss possible resolutions, and define the resolution
options.D: Define the conflict, confront the conflict, discuss possible resolutions, define the resolution, and acknowledge the conflict

questions.explanation: Acknowledge the conflict, define the conflict, confront the conflict, discuss possible resolutions, and define the resolution. Conflict resolution is the process of resolving, or ending, a conflict. During the first step, you recognize or admit that there is a conflict. During the second step, you examine the conflict and answer "who, why, what, when, where, and how." During the third step, you pick an appropriate time and place to discuss the conflict with the other person(s). During the fourth step, you try to find common ground between you and the other person(s) to find a possible resolution that you can both agree on. Finally, you choose a resolution that is mutually acceptable.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-EI-007—Stop the Madness (Conflict Resolution in Business)

questions.question: When people are motivated, they

options.D: tend to be unengaged at work. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 4

questions.explanation: Work hard to achieve success. When people are motivated, they work hard to achieve success. Motivated people do not focus on quantity over quality; rather, they are usually especially diligent in their work and take great pride in ensuring its quality. Motivated people take the necessary steps to make sure that their work is accomplished within the appropriate timeline. Motivated people also tend to be highly engaged at work compared to unengaged individuals.

questions.question: Jeni's company does not provide specific waste containers to encourage recycling. If she brings in her own container and begins using it, she is initiating change by
questions.explanation: Personal action. Jeni is initiating change through her actions. There is no evidence that she actively motivated others, communicated, or used teamwork to encourage recycling.

questions.question: A company's values, artifacts, and assumptions are all elements of its organizational
questions.explanation: Culture. An organization's values are the things it considers important and meaningful. Artifacts are observable things that demonstrate an organization's values. Assumptions are unspoken beliefs that underpin all of an organization's attitudes and actions. Values, artifacts, and assumptions are all elements of organizational culture. They are not elements of a company's rulebook, persona, or marketing efforts.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-EI-064—Culture Club (The Nature of Organizational Culture)

questions.question: Determining your personal net worth means finding out how much money you would have if you

options.A: sold all of your assets and took on more debts.
options.B: paid all of your debts and purchased more assets.
options.C: earned twice as much money as you made this year.
options.D: sold all of your assets and paid off all your debts.

questions.explanation: Sold all of your assets and paid off all your debts. Net worth is a single amount representing how much money you would have if you sold all of your assets and paid off all your debts. Net worth does not represent how much money you would have if you paid all of your debts and purchased more assets, earned twice as much money as you made this year, or sold all of your assets and took on more debts. http://money.howstuffworks.com/personal-finance/financial-planning/net-worth.htm
sources: SOURCE: McGrath, J. (2017). How to determine your net worth. Retrieved August 10, 2017, from

questions.question: An effective accounting system is

options.A: kept both electronically and on paper.

questions.explanation: Easy to use. An effective accounting system is easy to use. If it is hard to use, people might make mistakes or not be able to keep the information current. Effective accounting systems are kept electronically and frequently backed up so the information won't be lost. These systems don't need to be a major business expensive, as many affordable options are available. An effective accounting system should be flexible and expandable rather than unchangeable. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 18
sources: SOURCE: LAP-FI-085—Show Me the Money (Nature of Accounting)

questions.question: Which of the following is most likely to be a source of cash flowing into a business:
questions.explanation: A bank loan. A loan from a bank or investor is a source of cash flowing into a business that business owners can use to operate. A purchase of assets requires a business to spend money and represents outflow. Income tax is an expense the business must pay on any income earned. Owner's equity is an outflow of money to help the owner pay for personal expenses.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-FI-091—Count the Cash (Cash Flow Statements)

questions.question: What might happen if an organization does not have solid financial practices in place?

options.A: The organization's chain of command will be ignored.
options.B: Employees will have unlimited decision-making power.
options.C: Mergers and acquisitions will go much more smoothly.
options.D: It will struggle to make decisions and generate a profit.

questions.explanation: It will struggle to make decisions and generate a profit. Without solid financial practices, companies would struggle to make financially viable decisions and to make a profit. Employees will not necessarily have unlimited decision-making power because of a lack of solid financial practices. Mergers and acquisitions would most likely be more difficult rather than easier without solid financial practices. Finally, a lack of solid financial practices would not necessarily relate to whether or not the company's chain of command is followed.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-FI-355—Money Morals (The Role of Ethics in Finance)

questions.question: Zero-based budgeting requires managers to

options.B: demonstrate the need for every expense.
options.D: plan at least two years in advance.

questions.explanation: Demonstrate the need for every expense. Zero-based budgeting starts the budget over every fiscal year. Managers are required to demonstrate the need for every expense rather than just “rolling over” amounts from the previous budgeting period. Zero-based budgeting does not require managers to gather input from every employee, to document all income, or to plan two years in advance.

questions.question: A marketing-information system (MkIS) may be able to help marketers prevent a(n)
questions.explanation: Business crisis. A marketing-information system may be able to help marketers prevent a business crisis. Because they can constantly compare information to the business's plans and goals, marketers may be able to identify problems before they start. An MkIS is not likely to prevent a shipping error, an environmental violation, or an economic recession.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-IM-002—Get the Facts Straight (Marketing-Information Management)

questions.question: Which of the following is a true statement regarding collection of personal data:

options.A: Marketers should inform customers when collecting data.
options.B: Collecting personal data has no benefits for customers.
options.C: It's against the law to collect personal data.
options.D: Personal data are usually collected for unethical reasons. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 5

questions.explanation: Marketers should inform customers when collecting data. To balance the benefits and concerns of personal data collection, marketers should put ethical principles at the forefront. One of the ways they can do that is by openly informing consumers that their data are being collected. Collecting personal data does have benefits for customers; for example, it makes it easier for them to find the right products. It is not against the law to collect personal data. Finally, personal data are not usually collected for unethical reasons, although the collection of personal data does bring about many ethical conflicts.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-IM-025—Info With Integrity (Ethics in Marketing-Information Management)

questions.question: Market researchers often impose self-regulations that go beyond governmental regulations because

options.A: they can limit and control customer influence.
options.B: self-regulation leads to higher market share.
options.D: self-regulation helps to build trust with customers.

questions.explanation: Self-regulation helps to build trust with customers. Market researchers or market research organizations impose their own regulations that are not legally binding to guide their actions and decision-making. One reason that they do so is to build trust with customers, who will see the self-regulation as a sign that the market researchers care about protecting their privacy. Self-regulation does not necessarily lead to higher market share. Market researchers cannot overrule governmental regulations with their own self- regulations. Finally, market researchers do not impose self-regulations to limit and control customer influence. 2017, from https://iccwbo.org/global-issues-trends/responsible-business/marketing- advertising 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 19
sources: SOURCE: International Chamber of Commerce. (n.d.). Marketing and advertising. Retrieved October 4,

questions.question: For marketing research data to be useful, they must
questions.explanation: Be interpreted properly. For research data to be useful, they must be interpreted properly. The data may come from multiple sources, although multiple data sources are not always necessary. The specific nature of the research dictates which data sources are most appropriate. Bias is an inherent danger in research work and something to be avoided. Researchers' personal opinions sometimes impact the data, but reliable, accurate research is objective and free of bias. Although marketing research data are sometimes used to describe the target market, marketing research is also used to analyze the business's sales and market share, to forecast sales and trends, to be more competitive, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a discovery-oriented decision problem:

options.A: Why have sales of our most popular product dropped?
options.B: Which type of advertisement will best reach our target market?
options.C: What is the average age of our customer base?
options.D: How can we improve our business's market share?

questions.explanation: Why have sales of our most popular product dropped? A decision problem is the basic issue that a business's managers are facing. Discovery-oriented decision problems are aimed at answering "what" or "why." Asking why sales of a popular product have dropped is an example of a discovery-oriented decision problem. "Which type of advertisement will best reach our target market?" is an example of a strategy-oriented decision problem. "What is the average age of our customer base?" is an example of a research question. "How can we improve our business's market share?" is an example of a strategy- oriented decision problem.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-IM-282—What's the Problem? (Marketing Research Problems)

questions.question: The Calisto Café franchise calculated how many times its new menu item sold. What type of research data did the company collect?
questions.explanation: Quantitative. Calisto Café collected numeric data to determine how many times its new menu item sold. Therefore, it collected quantitative data, which are specific and measurable. The franchise did not collect qualitative data based on thoughts, opinions, feelings, or experiences. Exploratory and descriptive are types of research, not types of data.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-IM-284—Better by Design (Marketing Research Design)

questions.question: Marketers for a snack company want to research brand awareness for their new line of potato chips. They position researchers outside of a grocery store and stop passing customers to inquire about the product. This is an example of __________ sampling.
questions.explanation: Convenience. Convenience sampling is a non-probability sampling method in which researchers choose respondents based on opportunity. Stopping random customers at a grocery store is an example of convenience sampling because the participants are chosen based on the chance that they are walking by the researchers. Judgment sampling requires researchers to hand-pick respondents based on their knowledge or expertise. Simple random sampling gives every sampling unit a known and equal chance of being selected. Cluster sampling is a technique in which marketers divide the target population into non-overlapping subpopulations that are determined by geography.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-IM-016—Take Your Pick (Nature of Sampling Plans)

questions.question: Which of the following is an external source of data for a company's marketing decision-making:
questions.explanation: A competitor's television commercial. Observing competitors' marketing strategies in action is a useful way to gather data that can influence decision-making. It comes from outside of the organization itself, so it is an external source. A customer complaint record is an internal source, as is the company's marketing budget. A sales volume analysis allows the company to measure its actual sales against a number of different criteria. It is internal to the company rather than external.

questions.question: Qualitative marketing data are valuable, but they can be limited because

options.A: the data are objective rather than subjective.
options.B: participants' responses are constrained by limited options.
options.C: the data do not provide enough detail.
options.D: the data usually only include a small portion of the target market.

questions.explanation: The data usually only include a small portion of the target market. Qualitative data provide researchers with valuable knowledge to use in planning marketing activities. The scope, however, is limited. Since qualitative research usually only includes a very small portion of the target market, it is often not enough to make generalizations about an entire population. In qualitative data, participants' responses are not necessarily constrained by the limited options. Qualitative data tend to provide more detail than quantitative data. Finally, qualitative data are subjective rather than objective. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 20

questions.question: How can researchers limit the effects of their own personal biases when using observation as a data- collection method?

options.C: Use other research methods alongside observation

questions.explanation: Use other research methods alongside observation. Observation can be biased because the researcher is always filtering the data through his/her own personal biases and interpretations. Using other research methods to gather data can help to counteract these biases. Only observing certain populations is not effective or realistic. Staged observation studies do not necessarily reduce the effects of personal biases. Finally, obtaining permission from participants is an important part of ethical data collection, but it does not necessarily limit the effects of personal bias. Mason, OH: South-Western Cengage Learning.
sources: SOURCE: Zikmund, W.G., & Babin, B.J. (2010). Exploring market research (10th ed.) [pp. 248-250].

questions.question: A popular technology company just released a new smartphone that it believes will revolutionize the industry. After the product announcement, marketers set up automatic news and social media alerts to find out what people are saying about the new product. This is an example of

options.D: product/service management. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 6

questions.explanation: Buzz marketing. Buzz marketing is an analysis of social websites, blogs, and news to get a sense of how people feel about your company or product. Buzz marketing is often used when companies release a new product because it gives them a sense of how successful the product will be. Search engine optimization is the process of designing and submitting web pages so that they can be found easily and given a good position in search results. Direct marketing occurs when marketers work to get a specific, measurable action from a targeted group of consumers. Product/service management is a marketing function that involves obtaining, developing, maintaining, and improving a product or service mix in response to market opportunities. 2017, from http://www.wisegeek.net/what-is-buzz-monitoring.htm
sources: SOURCE: Queen, J., & Fritsky, L. (2017, September 16). What is buzz marketing? Retrieved October 5,

questions.question: Descriptive statistics are useful to market researchers because they

options.A: help to summarize data in a manageable way.
options.B: help researchers draw conclusions about a population.

questions.explanation: Help to summarize data in a manageable way. Descriptive statistics are used to describe the basic features of the data in a study. They help to summarize data in a way that is manageable and easy to understand. Descriptive statistics do not necessarily help researchers draw conclusions about a population; rather, inferential statistics allow market researchers to reach conclusions that go beyond the data itself. Descriptive statistics do not prevent researchers from losing detail; in fact, using descriptive statistics to represent data can eliminate some detail from a research study. Finally, descriptive statistics do not eliminate bias in research surveys. What's the difference? Retrieved October 5, 2017, from http://www.mymarketresearchmethods.com/descriptive-inferential-statistics-difference
sources: SOURCE: My Market Research Methods. (2015, October 16). Descriptive vs. inferential statistics:

questions.question: A company conducted a survey in which participants volunteered to answer questions about a controversial new product. When researchers analyzed the responses, they learned that all of the responses were either extremely positive or extremely negative. This is an example of how

options.B: survey results tend to be one-sided.
options.C: it is impossible to avoid errors.

questions.explanation: Voluntary responses can be biased. Voluntary response samples are surveys that allow participants to self-select. When people volunteer to participate in a sample, they usually have a strong opinion one way or another. If people are neutral about a topic, they are less likely to participate. Therefore, the results can be skewed. In general, survey results do not tend to be one-sided. It is not impossible to avoid errors if a survey is well designed. Finally, social desirability does affect survey responses, but this example is not related to this concept. 2017, from https://www.ma.utexas.edu/users/mks/statmistakes/biasedsampling.html 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 21
sources: SOURCE: Smith, M.K. (2012, August 28). Biased sampling and extrapolation. Retrieved October 5,

questions.question: When writing qualitative survey questions, it is important to

options.B: write as many questions as possible.
options.C: avoid questions that result in “yes/no” answers.
options.D: guide respondents to answer in a certain way.

questions.explanation: Avoid questions that result in “yes/no” answers. When writing qualitative survey questions, researchers should be sure to write questions that provoke a thoughtful, insightful response. Qualitative questions that ask a “yes” or “no” question do not tend to provide enough detail. Writing as many questions as possible is not necessarily a good strategy. Researchers will get more thorough response from a few well-written questions, and participants might get tired of answering questions thoughtfully if there are too many. Using complex wording and structure can confuse people and interfere with the quality of data. Finally, researchers should not guide respondents to answer in a certain way because leading respondents to a particular answer will skew the data. 2017, from http://www.custominsight.com/articles/effective-survey-questions.asp
sources: SOURCE: Custom Insight. (2017). Writing effective employee survey questions. Retrieved October 5,

questions.question: Marketing functions are interrelated and similar in purpose, which means that

options.A: only some of them are necessary.
options.B: they all need to work together.
options.C: they all include the same activities.
options.D: different functions work for different products.

questions.explanation: They all need to work together. Marketing functions are all important and have to work together to accomplish goals and objectives. All of the marketing functions are necessary to be successful. All marketing functions do not include the same activities. Also, while different functions might be more impactful than others for different products, this does not relate to the fact that all marketing functions are interrelated and similar in purpose.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-MK-001—Work the Big Seven (Marketing Functions)

questions.question: What action might marketers take to achieve a pricing objective of getting their product into more customers' hands?
questions.explanation: Lower their prices. Price, the amount of money a firm asks for in exchange for its products, is an element of the marketing mix. If marketers want to get a product into more customers' hands, they might lower the price. Asking a higher price would likely not help them achieve this objective. Offering more or fewer product features would be part of the product element of the marketing mix, not the price element.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-MP-002—Pick the Mix (Nature of Marketing Strategies)

questions.question: Alex is an 18-year-old high school student who enjoys soccer and music. Both of his parents are highly educated with professional careers. Which of the following details about Alex is an example of psychographic information:

options.C: He is a high school student.

questions.explanation: He enjoys soccer and music. Lifestyle interests such as soccer and music are examples of psychographic information; all of the other details given relate to demographics (e.g., age, gender, life stage, socioeconomic status).

questions.question: Why is it important to track the effectiveness of marketing efforts?

options.A: It provides data about the target market.
options.B: When a strategy is not effective, companies will halt all marketing efforts.
options.C: Being able to demonstrate success is essential to obtaining funding or moving forward.
options.D: It makes goals easier to remember.

questions.explanation: Being able to demonstrate success is essential to obtaining funding or moving forward. Marketing plans allow progress to be tracked in a measurable way, which is often necessary to demonstrate to upper management the need for funding or to continue with marketing efforts. Companies should not necessarily halt all marketing efforts if one strategy is not effective. Though marketing plans allow marketers to gather information about the target market better and to more easily remember goals, these benefits are not specifically linked to tracking the effectiveness of marketing efforts.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-MP-007—A Winning Plan (Nature of Marketing Plans)

questions.question: After performing environmental scanning, Discount Divas Department Store determined that its top competitor was shutting two of its stores in a certain city. Discount Divas Department Store then decided to open a store in the same area. This is an example of how

options.A: it is important to focus on threats rather than opportunities.
options.B: situation analysis should focus on internal issues rather than external issues.
options.C: environmental scanning can reveal new business opportunities.
options.D: a SWOT analysis should be completed after the market planning process. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 7

questions.explanation: Environmental scanning can reveal new business opportunities. Environmental scanning is collecting information about the environment surrounding your business. It includes being aware of what competitors are doing and acting accordingly. Discount Divas Department Store planned to open a new store in a certain area because of the lack of competition that it uncovered during environmental scanning. Situation analysis should focus on both internal and external issues. It is also important to focus on both threats and opportunities. Finally, a SWOT analysis should be completed during the market planning process because the information that a SWOT analysis provides should inform the planning process. https://www.slideshare.net/haruhi_13/environmental-scanning-2 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 22
sources: SOURCE: Slideshare. (2011, July 23). Environmental scanning. Retrieved October 10, 2017, from

questions.question: Dale has worked in the IT department at his company for 15 years. Every Friday, he enters information into a database to record any technology issues that came up that week. This week, however, his computer alerts him that its new operating system is no longer compatible with the data-entry software. This is a common issue related to information storage called
questions.explanation: Obsolescence of format. One negative result of storing/recording information is the possibility of obsolescence of format or medium, which occurs when an old technology is no longer functional. This situation is an example of obsolescence of format because Dale's old software was no longer able to function on his new computer's operating system. Elimination of data, excess storage loss, and technology audit failure are not terms used to describe possible negative results of storing information. from https://wiki.dlib.indiana.edu/display/DIGIPRES/Media+Format+Obsolescence
sources: SOURCE: Dowding, H. (2016, September 28). Media format obsolescence. Retrieved August 15, 2017,

questions.question: The basic structure of a web page is typically dictated by
questions.explanation: HTML. HTML, or HyperText Markup Language, is the basic language that provides the structure for a web page. HTML is then modified and enhanced by other technologies and languages such as JavaScript. CDML is not a type of web language. The basic structure of a web page is not dictated by online visitors. 10, 2017, from https://blog.hubspot.com/marketing/web-design-html-css- javascript#sm.001qvoxy11ebkfnptoy1u7m9vr7lb
sources: SOURCE: Hubspot. (2017). Web design 101: How HTML, CSS, and JavaScript work. Retrieved August

questions.question: To help ensure that its records will survive a large scale disaster such as a flood or hurricane, the ABC Company should store its records
questions.explanation: In a remote location. Storing records in a remote location far from the business can help protect information if a major disaster would strike. Storing records in a locked file room helps achieve security, but would be of little help if a tornado destroyed the building they were in. Records stored on desktop computers would not survive a disaster unless the records were regularly backed up to a remote location. Storing records in digital formats can enable the easy transmission of data to remote locations, but if the data were not stored remotely before tragedy struck, there would be little that could be done to recover them.

questions.question: The mean is the average of all numbers in a data set, whereas the median is

options.A: the middle number in a sequence.
options.B: the number that occurs most often.
options.C: the lowest number in a data set.
options.D: the number that occurs least often.

questions.explanation: The middle number in a sequence. The statistical mean is the middle number in a sequence of numbers, not the lowest number in a data set. The number that occurs most often is called the mode. There is no term to describe the number that occurs least often in a data set. from http://searchdatacenter.techtarget.com/definition/statistical-mean-median-mode-and- range
sources: SOURCE: Rouse, M. (2017). Statistical mean, median, mode and range. Retrieved August 10, 2017,

questions.question: Ruined reputation, lost revenue, and data leaks are all possible consequences of a(n)
questions.explanation: Information security breach. Possible negative consequences of an information security breach include a ruined company reputation, lost revenue, and data leaks. These are not likely consequences of an employee promotion or a country-wide product recall. A sexual assault scandal may cause harm to a company's reputation, but it probably wouldn't result in significant loss of revenue or data leaks. Retrieved August 15, 2017, from https://www.besttechie.com/5-consequences-information- security-breach
sources: SOURCE: Best Techie. (2011, November 2). 5 consequences of an information security breach.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a tangible resource that you might need for a project:
questions.explanation: Paint brushes. Tangible resources are the equipment, supplies, materials, and facilities needed to complete a project. Paint brushes are a tangible resource that could come in handy for a variety of projects. Volunteers are a human resource rather than a tangible resource. Wages are a financial resource rather than a tangible resource. Time is a dimension of human resources. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 23
sources: SOURCE: LAP-OP-531—Get What You Need (Identifying Project Resources)

questions.question: When selecting vendors, you should always look for organizations that have

options.A: the smallest catalogue of products or services.
options.B: the same political beliefs as you or your organization.
options.C: positive testimonials and several years of experience.
options.D: higher prices than any other vendors in the area.

questions.explanation: Positive testimonials and several years of experience. It is important to do your research when selecting vendors for your business. You should always look for vendors with positive testimonials and several years of experience. It is not necessary or relevant to select vendors with the same political beliefs as you or your organization. You should select vendors with large catalogues of products and services, not small catalogues. This will allow you to choose from a wide variety of products as your business grows. You should look for vendors with competitive pricing instead of vendors with the highest prices in the area. criteria. Retrieved August 15, 2017, from http://www.purchasing-procurement- center.com/selecting-a-vendor.html
sources: SOURCE: Purchasing & Procurement Center. (2017). Selecting a vendor: Your vendor selection

questions.question: Cassidy leaves work early every day to get to basketball practice on time, but she still reports her full scheduled hours. What consequence is her employer likely to face as a result of Cassidy's actions?
questions.explanation: Increased payroll cost. Reporting more time than actually worked will increase the business's payroll cost. There's no indication that Cassidy's action would produce poor customer reviews. Productivity would be reduced, not improved, by Cassidy leaving early.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-OP-025—Buck Busters (Employee Role in Expense Control)

questions.question: Fostering efficiency in the production process is a goal of

options.D: information technology. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 8

questions.explanation: Supply chain management. A supply chain is a complex system that links manufacturers, distributers, and customers. One of the primary goals of supply chain management is to foster efficiency in the production process so that goods can be readily available for customers when they need them. This is not a primary goal of managerial accounting, human resources management, or information technology. Managerial accounting is the process of analyzing financial information to help achieve organizational goals. Human resources management is a system for overseeing and protecting all the people within an organization. Information technology refers to the maintenance and use of an organization's computer systems, software, networks, and all other technologies. chain. Retrieved August 15, 2017, from http://www.informit.com/articles/article.aspx?p=2166717&seqNum=4
sources: SOURCE: Haozhe, C., Defee, C., Gibson, B., & Hanna, J. (2014, January 10). Defining the supply

questions.question: Brendon has worked as a journalist at a local newspaper for six months. He likes his job but hasn't received much feedback on his work. He's concerned that his boss isn't happy with his performance, but he isn't sure what he should do. Which of Brendon's employee rights is being violated?
questions.explanation: Right to expect criticism. Employees have several basic workplace rights that must be fulfilled, including the right to expect criticism. Employees who are never given effective feedback are unlikely to succeed in the long term. Employees also have the right to a safe environment, a right to fair treatment, and a right to expect recognition, but these rights are not violated in this example. feedback. Retrieved August 16, 2017, from https://clearconceptinc.ca/things-not-better-left- unsaid-the-importance-of-employee-feedback
sources: SOURCE: Williams, L. (2016, April 11). Things not better left unsaid: The importance of employee

questions.question: Which of the following guidelines for writing cover letters is true:

options.A: Do not include your job qualifications and experience in your cover letter.
options.B: In your cover letter, repeat the exact information from your résumé.
options.C: Include personal information like your marital status in your cover letter.
options.D: Customize your cover letter to the position you are applying for.

questions.explanation: Customize your cover letter to the position you are applying for. Cover letters are intended to give employers a better sense of your unique skills and experiences and should be customized to each specific job. They should not repeat the exact same information from your résumé; rather, they should elaborate on the information from your résumé. It would be inappropriate to include personal information like your marital status in a cover letter. You should include your job qualifications and experience in a cover letter. https://www.thebalance.com/cover-letters-4073661 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 24
sources: SOURCE: Doyle, A. (2017, July 19). Cover letters. Retrieved August 16, 2017, from

questions.question: Even though Joseph has worked as an accountant for 20 years, he recently enrolled in a community college course to help him master the new software his department is using. He knows that if he doesn't keep up with current technologies, he could be replaced by a younger worker. This is an example of the importance of __________ in the workplace.
questions.explanation: Ongoing education. For employees like Joseph, ongoing education is a way to enhance current skills and gain new skills that can improve employment opportunities and personal achievement. This situation is not an example of the importance of mentorship programs or student loan forgiveness; however, both are beneficial in the workplace. Frequent training is also important at work, but is not demonstrated in this example because Joseph received his additional training outside of the workplace. 2017, from https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/benefits-continuing-education-everyone-ian- cohen
sources: SOURCE: Cohen, I. (2015, July 9). Benefits of continuing education for everyone. Retrieved August 16,

questions.question: Eliza and her boss, Melissa, don't get along very well. So when Eliza had a question about an important new project she was working on last week, she went straight to Melissa's boss instead of asking Melissa first. Melissa was furious; she hadn't told her boss about the new project yet, and he was upset when he heard about it from one of Melissa's employees. In other words, Eliza broke
questions.explanation: The chain of command. Chain of command is an organizational structure that outlines who reports to whom. It is important to follow the chain of command to avoid situations like this. When Eliza went over Melissa's head to ask about a project, she broke her company's chain of command, not her project's protocol or local employment law. Links of authority is not a term used to describe organizational structure. Retrieved August 15, 2017, from http://smallbusiness.chron.com/importance-following-chain- command-business-23560.html
sources: SOURCE: Kelchner, L. (2017). The importance of following the chain of command in business.

questions.question: At Paul's job, he's in charge of building a positive public image for his company. He develops relationships with the public and media and manages crises that may threaten his company's reputation. Which of the following marketing careers does Paul have:
questions.explanation: Public relations. Public relations professionals are in charge of building a positive public image for their companies. Public relations professionals can write and send out press releases, schedule and run press conferences, communicate and develop relationships with the public and media, and manage crises that may threaten reputations. Sales professionals respond to customer needs and wants through planned, personalized communications intended to influence purchase decisions and ensure satisfaction. Marketing researchers are in charge of figuring out what customers need and want and why they do what they do. Product or brand management professionals are in charge of monitoring and developing one or more existing products or brands.

questions.question: Online pricing tools can help a business

options.A: reduce the need for customer service.
options.C: beat the prices of larger online retailers.

questions.explanation: Track competitors' pricing. Online pricing tools help businesses track the prices of their competitors on a regular basis. Then, businesses can adjust their prices accordingly. While online pricing tools can help business obtain customers, they do not reduce the need to provide high-quality customer service. Online pricing tools do not ensure that a business will beat the prices of larger online retailers; the larger companies will likely continue to lower their prices in response. Finally, online pricing tools are not necessarily related to a business's target markets. business. Retrieved October 5, 2017, from http://upstreamcommerce.com/blog/2011/10/10/3-ways-automated-pricing-systems-harm- online-retail-business
sources: SOURCE: Miller, G. (2011, October 10). 3 ways automated pricing systems can harm your online retail

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of illegal price discrimination:

options.A: Members of a store's loyalty program receive discounts.
options.B: A retailer sends catalogs with higher prices to higher income areas.
options.C: One paper towel roll is $2; six paper towel rolls are $9.
options.D: A café charges higher prices when customers are from different countries.

questions.explanation: A café charges higher prices when customers are from different countries. Price discrimination is usually legal, unless that discrimination is done on the basis of race, religion, nationality, or gender. A café cannot legally charge higher prices to customers who are from different countries. However, retailers can set different prices based on income bracket. The paper towel prices are an example of bulk discounts, which are legal. Finally, store loyalty programs that offer discounts are not an example of illegal price discrimination. October 5, 2017, from https://econsultancy.com/blog/64068-what-is-price-discrimination-and- is-it-ethical 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 25
sources: SOURCE: Shpanya, A. (2014, January 6). What is price discrimination and is it ethical? Retrieved

questions.question: Before setting pricing objectives, a company should set its __________ objectives.

options.D: competitor's 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 9

questions.explanation: Marketing. A company should set its marketing objectives before its pricing objectives because the business must know where it wants to go before choosing selling prices to get it there. Markup is the difference between the cost of a product and its selling price. Profit-oriented pricing objectives focus on creating profits for the business and are a specific type of pricing objective. A company cannot set its competitor's objectives.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PI-003—Make Cents (Factors Affecting Selling Price)

questions.question: During which stage of a product's life cycle does the manufacturer try to maintain the market share that it has obtained?
questions.explanation: Maturity stage. During the maturity stage, a product is well established within the market and the manufacturer's goal is to maintain the product's market share. The introduction stage occurs when a product is first introduced to the market. The decline stage occurs when a product's market begins to shrink. The growth stage takes place after the initial introduction phase and is generally characterized by growth in sales and profits. http://productlifecyclestages.com
sources: SOURCE: Living Better Media. (2017). Product life cycle stages. Retrieved September 25, 2017, from

questions.question: The use of technology in product/service management can help companies
questions.explanation: Monitor the success of products. Technology in product/service management can help companies monitor the status of their products so that their marketing efforts are well informed and relevant to what each product needs. Technology in product/service management cannot help companies hire more effective employees, obtain secrets from competitors, or resolve human resource issues. http://www.autodeskfusionlifecycle.com/en
sources: SOURCE: Autodesk. (2017). Product development accelerated. Retrieved September 25, 2017, from

questions.question: The Zytec Company has designed a product to become obsolete, or unusable, before it should reasonably need replacement. Zytec is implementing
questions.explanation: Planned obsolescence. Planned obsolescence is the practice of designing a product to become obsolete, or unusable, before it should reasonably need replacement. This practice is not referred to as determined uselessness, failure by company design, or deliberate mortality.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PM-040—Safe and Sound (Ethics in Product/Service Management)

questions.question: Matthew, a product manager at a technology company, recently came up with a brand new packaging method for the laptops that his company sells. His new method would require significantly fewer materials than the old method, helping his company reduce its ecological footprint. Matthew's new packaging method is an example of
questions.explanation: Innovation. Innovation is the introduction or creation of something new. Intelligence is the ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills. Production is the action of making or manufacturing. Brainstorming is the process of solving a problem or producing an idea through group discussion. Retrieved October 3, 2017, from https://www.ideatovalue.com/inno/nickskillicorn/2016/03/innovation-15-experts-share- innovation-definition
sources: SOURCE: Idea to Value. (2017). What is innovation? 15 experts share their innovation definition.

questions.question: Which of the following creative thinking techniques includes the use of metaphors and similes:
questions.explanation: Forced analogies. Forced analogies are a type of forced relations that create connections where none currently exist using metaphors or similes. Morphological analysis is a technique that combines a product's attributes in different ways to create new products. Free association is a technique that involves allowing your mind to wander and recording your flow of ideas to make connections. Forced questioning is a technique that uses basic question starters to develop original questions about a product opportunity. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 26
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PM-127—Unleash Your Mind (Techniques for Generating Product Ideas)

questions.question: A company sells its below-standard clothing at outlet stores, while it sells its higher grade clothing in department stores. The company uses grades and standards to

options.A: indicate where the product will be sold.
options.B: indicate how the product can be used.

questions.explanation: Indicate where the product will be sold. Manufacturers often use product grades to determine where they will offer their products for sale. Apparel manufacturers might sell below-standard items through discount outlets rather than through department stores. Standards and grades can also be used to indicate how the product can be used, help salespeople, and indicate product safety, but these uses are not illustrated in this example.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PM-008—Raise the Bar (Grades and Standards)

questions.question: Chloe purchased some wireless headphones with Bluetooth capability. Unfortunately, the headphones failed to pair with her Bluetooth-enabled smartphone. Chloe has the right to return the headphones because they carry a(n) __________ warranty.
questions.explanation: Implied. Since the headphones have Bluetooth capability, Chloe has the right to expect that they will function with Bluetooth-enabled devices such as her smartphone. There is no indication that the headphones came with an express warranty, either full or limited.

questions.question: Oven Fresh is a specialty food company that sells a variety of baked goods, baking mixes, and cookbooks. These items make up Oven Fresh's

options.D: market specialty. 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 10

questions.explanation: Product mix. The particular assortment of products a business offers to meet its market's needs and its company's goals is its product mix. A product line, on the other hand, is a group of related product items. The product items in a product line are somewhat like the members of a family in their relationship to each other; they often resemble each other in some way. Market offering and market specialty are not terms used to describe the assortment of products that a business sells.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PM-003—Mix and Match (The Nature of the Product Mix)

questions.question: Quantum Cable customers can get Internet, phone, and TV service for a single low price. This is an example of
questions.explanation: Product bundling. Product bundling occurs when a company sells a group of products for a lower price than if the consumer purchased each product separately. One common “bundle” includes Internet, phone, and TV service. Consumer demand, product separation, and combined marketing are not terms used to describe product bundling. http://smallbusiness.chron.com/bundle-pricing-strategy-67049.html
sources: SOURCE: Merritt, C. (2017). Bundle pricing strategy. Retrieved September 26, 2017, from

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a brand mark:
questions.explanation: Apple's apple symbol. A brand mark is a distinctive symbol, design, sound, or group of letters that is seen or heard but cannot be spoken, such as Apple's “apple” symbol that appears on all of its products. The phrase “Kellogg's Frosted Flakes” is an example of a brand name—the part of a brand that can be spoken, such as a word, a phrase, a letter, a number, or any combination of these. Nike's phrase “Just do it.” is a brand slogan, not a brand mark. The name “Ford Motors” is an example of a trade name—the name that identifies a company.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PM-006—It's a Brand, Brand, Brand World! (Nature of Product Branding)

questions.question: Alcon Technology conducts research on its target market to discover what customers do and don't like about the company. Alcon then uses this feedback to improve the company's branding efforts. Alcon is using __________ in branding.
questions.explanation: Customer voice. By listening to customer experiences and opinions through research and outreach, companies can better align their business activities to the needs of their market. In this example, Alcon Technology is using customer voice to improve its branding efforts—not internal feedback, employee insights, or company history. success. Retrieved September 27, 2017, from http://www.aberdeenessentials.com/cmo- essentials/the-voice-of-the-customer-in-branding-a-simple-formula-for-success 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 27
sources: SOURCE: Minkara, O. (2015, March 30). The voice of the customer in branding: A simple formula for

questions.question: PetFast pet store is known for its low prices and great value. The store frequently advertises its weekly sales, during which customers can get bulk pet foods for discounted prices. This is an example of corporate
questions.explanation: Positioning. Corporate positioning involves using marketing methods to convince target consumers to buy from the company instead of the competition. In this case, PetFast used its low prices as its positioning strategy to attract budget-conscious customers. Corporate modeling, price fixing, and strategizing are not terms used to describe positioning. http://smallbusiness.chron.com/corporate-positioning-strategy-64502.html
sources: SOURCE: Markgraf, B. (2017). A corporate positioning strategy. Retrieved September 26, 2017, from

questions.question: Marcy would like to learn more about VaccuFresh vacuum cleaners, so she goes to the company's website and begins a live chat with a service representative. From VaccuFresh's perspective, Marcy's online chat is a(n)
questions.explanation: Customer touchpoint. A customer touchpoint is any encounter where a business and a customer engage. In this example, Marcy's live chat with a service representative would be considered a customer touchpoint to VaccuFresh—not a marketing strategy, customer analysis, or advertising success. http://360connext.com/customer-touchpoint
sources: SOURCE: Walters, J. (2017). What is a customer touchpoint? Retrieved September 26, 2017, from

questions.question: A pizza place advertises its organic toppings and extended delivery range. This is an example of what type of institutional promotion?
questions.explanation: Patronage. Patronage promotions are designed to promote a firm's prestige or its features. Presenting information about the firm's features that will benefit the customer is another way to sell the firm for non- product reasons. For example, patronage promotions for a pizza place might tell customers about its organic toppings and extended delivery range. Public relations promotions are a type of institutional promotion created to deal with controversial public issues that are related to a company or its products. Public service promotions inform customers about noncontroversial issues that are in the public's best interest. Primary is a type of product promotion.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PR-004—Know Your Options (Product and Institutional Promotion)

questions.question: Catalogs, telemarketing, and email are examples of
questions.explanation: Direct marketing. Direct marketing is a form of advertising that includes communication straight from the organization to the consumer without the use of mass media. Direct marketing efforts are usually more personalized and targeted than other forms of advertising. Catalogs, telemarketing, and email are all examples of direct marketing because they go straight from the company to the individual who is being targeted. Catalogs, telemarketing, and email are not examples of sponsorship, product placement, or guerrilla marketing.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PR-001—Spread the Word (Nature of Promotional Mix)

questions.question: Which of the following is both a positive and a negative effect that technology has had on promotion:

options.D: Greater interactivity with customers 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 11

questions.explanation: Greater interactivity with customers. Technology has changed promotion by allowing communication to be two sided, rather than just from the marketer to the customer. Using platforms such as social media, customers can respond to promotional efforts. This is a positive effect because it allows businesses to get instant feedback and build positive rapport with customers. It also has a negative effect because customers can share their feedback publically—and when it is negative feedback, it can be harmful to a company's reputation. Difficulty adjusting to new platforms is a negative effect that technology has had on promotion, but it is not necessarily positive. Convenience of online payment options doesn't necessarily relate to promotion. Finally, technology has increased the number of media outlets available for promotion. Retrieved October 9, 2017, from http://smallbusiness.chron.com/positive-negative-impact- digital-media-business-20910.html 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 28
sources: SOURCE: Morely, M. (2011, August 30). The positive & negative impact of digital media on business.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a deceptive promotional practice:

options.A: Requiring all salespeople to adhere to company ethics policies while on the job
options.B: Representing goods and services accurately and truthfully in sales materials
options.C: Advertising goods even though there isn't enough stock to meet expected demand
options.D: Publishing a job listing even though the position will be filled by a current employee

questions.explanation: Advertising goods even though there isn't enough stock to meet expected demand. Advertising for goods or services when a company knows it will not have enough inventory to meet expected demand is unethical and deceptive. Representing goods and services accurately and truthfully and requiring salespeople to adhere to company ethics policies are both examples of ethical sales practices. Publishing a job listing even though the position will be filled by a current employee may be unethical and deceptive, but it's not an example of a sales practice. from http://statelaws.findlaw.com/consumer-laws/details-on-state-deceptive-trade- practices.html
sources: SOURCE: FindLaw. (2017). Details on state deceptive trade practices. Retrieved September 27, 2017,

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a business magazine:
questions.explanation: H.R. Magazine. Business magazines appeal primarily to members of the business community. H.R. Magazine is an example of a business magazine. Sports Illustrated, Us Weekly, and Seventeen are all considered consumer magazines.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-PR-003—Ad-Quipping Your Business (Types of Advertising Media)

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a pull sales promotion strategy:

options.B: A booth at a trade show
options.D: A buy one, get one free deal

questions.explanation: A buy one, get one free deal. A pull sales promotion strategy involves a producer promoting its products directly to the consumer. Offering a buy one, get one free deal is an example of this because it targets the consumer and encourages demand for the product. Push sales promotion strategies involve a producer promoting its products to intermediaries, who then promote the products to the consumers. A booth at a trade show is a push sales promotion strategy because it allows a company to expose its products to other manufacturers, retailers, and wholesalers. A sales incentive program is also an example of push strategy because it encourages salespeople to increase promotional efforts rather than target the consumer. A slotting allowance is a cash premium paid to a retailer to cover the costs of displaying a manufacturer's products on its shelves. It encourages the retailer to sell certain products, so it is a push strategy. Columbus, OH: Glencoe/McGraw-Hill.
sources: SOURCE: Farese, L.S., Kimbrell, G., & Woloszyk, C.A. (2012). Marketing essentials (pp. 403-411).

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of using public relations over other promotional techniques:
questions.explanation: Lower cost. Public relations efforts tend to be less expensive than other promotional tactics such as advertising. Marketers have less control over the delivery of public relations than they do over other channels, which is a disadvantage rather than an advantage. Public relations efforts are also affected by external events because a planned public relations effort can be bumped from the media if a major event occurs. This is a disadvantage of public relations. Finally, public relations activities tend to have greater reach because they can be picked up by a large number of media. https://www.knowthis.com/public-relations/advantages-of-public-relations
sources: SOURCE: KnowThis.com. (2017). Advantages of public relations. Retrieved October 9, 2017, from

questions.question: Which part of an advertisement inspires a sense of urgency?
questions.explanation: Call to action. A call to action is a statement that urges viewers to take action in order to benefit from whatever the advertisement is offering. It usually includes clear, concise instructions for what people should do. Contact information, the headline, and the image are all important parts of an advertisement, but they are not intended to inspire a sense of urgency. 2017, from http://yourbusiness.azcentral.com/7-elements-print-advertising-5911.html 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 29
sources: SOURCE: Erickson, A. (2016, September 29). 7 elements of print advertising. Retrieved October 9,

questions.question: Ollie is designing a magazine ad, and he wants to draw attention to one main feature. To do so, Ollie should
questions.explanation: Use white space. White space is blank space in a print advertisement that is unoccupied by text or illustrations. White space helps to put focus on a specific aspect of an advertisement. Ollie could use white space to draw attention to one main feature in his ad. Changing fonts often is not necessarily a good design practice. Keeping his audience in mind and avoiding clashing colors are good design practices for Ollie to follow, but they are not necessarily related to drawing attention to a specific feature of an ad. 2017, from https://designschool.canva.com/blog/design-rules
sources: SOURCE: Stribely, M. (2015, April 14). 20 design rules you should never break. Retrieved October 9,

questions.question: Which of the following is a one-way public relations tool:
questions.explanation: Press releases. Press releases are written information provided to the media in order to obtain publicity. A press release is a one-way tool because it does not offer interactivity between the marketers and the receivers. Sponsorship is an agreement that allows a company to pay a fee to a team, event, etc. for the right to affiliate itself with that team, event, etc. Press conferences are meetings to which a business or group invites members of the media in order to make an announcement. Media tours occur when a company spokesperson travels to key cities to introduce a new product. Sponsorships, press conferences, and media tours are all interactive public relations tools. October 9, 2017, from https://open.lib.umn.edu/principlesmarketing/chapter/12-1-public- relations-activities-and-tools
