sources: SOURCE: University of Minnesota Libraries. (2010). Public relations activities and tools. Retrieved

questions.question: An airline experienced a mechanical issue that was widely publicized, and the company is now worried about how sales will be affected. Who should be the target audience of their public relations efforts?
questions.explanation: Customers. If the airline is worried about how its mechanical issue will affect sales, it should use public relations efforts to target customers and counteract any negative publicity. Public relations efforts can be used to target investors, community members, or business partners, but these should not necessarily be used if the airline is worried about sales. http://www.referenceforbusiness.com/small/Op-Qu/Public-Relations.html
sources: SOURCE: Reference for Business. (2017). Public relations. Retrieved October 9, 2017, from

questions.question: RunRight and QuickStride both sell the same type of running shoes and apparel at similar prices. Jamie, however, prefers QuickStride because the employees always offer to help her find the correct size for her feet. She rarely shops at RunRight. QuickStride is using its __________ to beat its competition.

options.D: customer service 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM 12

questions.explanation: Customer service. You may think that if you offer a product with the best price and the best quality, you'll beat all the competition. Unfortunately, price and quality can be easily matched among competitors. Customer service is where the real competition among businesses begins. This example demonstrates the importance of excellent customer service, not product quality, price matching, or employee discipline.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-130—Go Beyond the Sale (Customer Service in Selling)

questions.question: Which of the following is a result of building and maintaining a loyal clientele:
questions.explanation: Reduced selling costs. Making the first sale to a new customer can cost a company a lot of money. Between advertising, determining wants and needs, and building a new relationship, the costs add up quickly. Once businesses have built a loyal clientele, they can cut back on some of these expensive activities—reducing their overall selling costs. Maintaining a loyal clientele increases, not decreases, sales volume because loyal customers return to buy on a regular basis. This leads to increased income and profit—not lower income and profit.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-115—Keep Them Loyal (Key Factors in Building Clientele)

questions.question: A CRM system can help salespeople
questions.explanation: Keep track of their customers. CRM (Customer Relationship Management) systems allow salespeople and their companies to organize and keep track of their relationships with customers and other relevant stakeholders. CRM systems are not intended to help salespeople manage their personal budgets, monitor their company's stock, or improve their personal selling skills. https://www.salesforce.com/eu/learning-centre/crm/what-is-crm 2018 HS ICDC MARKETING CLUSTER EXAM—KEY 30
sources: SOURCE: Salesforce.com, Inc. (2000-2017). What is CRM? Retrieved September 27, 2017, from

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a deceptive sales practice:

options.B: Paying customers to provide product testimonials
options.C: Selling credit cards over the phone
options.D: Charging a higher price than what was advertised

questions.explanation: Charging a higher price than what was advertised. If a salesperson promises to sell his/her products for a certain price and then charges more when the customer makes a purchase, s/he is being deceptive and is likely breaking the law. While it is not necessarily deceptive to pay customers for testimonials, the payment must be disclosed so that consumers are aware of it. Selling credit cards over the phone and offering a money-back guarantee are not deceptive sales practices. 2017, from https://www.ftc.gov/tips-advice/business-center/advertising-and- marketing/advertising-and-marketing-basics
sources: SOURCE: Federal Trade Commission. (n.d.). Advertising and marketing basics. Retrieved October 10,

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of product information that is useful in selling:

options.B: How the product should be used

questions.explanation: How the product should be used. One of the most important things that a salesperson should know is how his/her products can and should be used. It is very difficult for customers to understand how a product will benefit them if they don't know how to use it. The manufacturer's company history and the salesperson's employee experience are not relevant to a customer's buying decision. It would be unwise for a salesperson to share the most common product defects with a customer unless the customer asks.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-131—Get Informed (Acquiring Product Information for Use in Selling)

questions.question: BeGone laundry stain remover is the only product on the market that can completely remove permanent marker stains without damaging your clothes. This is an example of a(n) __________ benefit.
questions.explanation: Unique. Unique or exclusive benefits are advantages that are only available from your good, service, or business. Since BeGone stain remover is the only product on the market that can completely remove permanent marker stains without damaging your clothes, BeGone has a unique advantage over its competition. Obvious or apparent benefits are advantages that need little explanation by the salesperson because the customer can easily recognize the benefit. Hidden benefits are advantages that cannot be seen or understood without the assistance of a salesperson. “Noticeable” is not a term used to describe product benefits.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-113—Find Features, Boost Benefits (Feature-Benefit Selling)

questions.question: Brandon is a new salesperson at a local car dealership. To be most successful at his job, what should Brandon do before attempting to make any sales?

options.A: Become an expert on the cars he will be selling
options.B: Establish relationships with his fellow salespeople
options.C: Prospect and qualify leads for potential customers
options.D: Spend a day shadowing a more experienced salesperson

questions.explanation: Become an expert on the cars he will be selling. No matter what product s/he is selling, no salesperson can sell without preparation. The first thing Brandon should do before making any sales is to learn about the cars he will be selling, so that he can effectively help his customers find cars that fit their specific needs. Establishing relationships with fellow salespeople is beneficial, but not necessary to make sales. Prospecting and qualifying leads is something Brandon should do after learning about the cars he will be selling. Shadowing a more experienced salesperson would also be beneficial to Brandon, but it is more important for him to learn about his products first.
sources: SOURCE: LAP-SE-048—Set Your Sales (The Selling Process)

questions.question: Shannon wants to buy a new purse because her current purse is old and out of style, and she wants other people to think she is fashionable. Shannon is motivated by a(n) __________ reward.
questions.explanation: External. When an individual is motivated by an external reward, s/he acts out of a desire for a reward that comes from an external source rather than an internal source. Shannon's desire for peer approval is an example of an external reward, not an internal reward. Unethical and shallow are not words used to describe types of rewards. September 28, 2017, from https://www.jeremysaid.com/blog/the-surprising-science-behind- customer-motivation $10.00 | ICEMK18 100 QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS AS USED AT DECA’S 2018 INTERNATIONAL CAREER DEVELOPMENT CONFERENCE 2018 PRACTICE EXAM BOOKLET PERSONAL FINANCIAL LITERACY
sources: SOURCE: Jeremy Said. (2017). The surprising science behind customer motivation. Retrieved
